companies.name: Apple Inc
companies.summary: Apple edged up about 0.9% today to $300.65, trading within a fairly tight intraday range and holding comfortably above its previous close. companies.name: Microsoft Corp
companies.summary: Microsoft slipped 2.3% today to $370.71, closing well below its prior session and near the bottom of its intraday range, which may reflect broader market pressure or some caution following CEO Satya Nadella's public warnings about AI concentration risks — a notable signal given how central AI growth is to the bull case for the stock. At the same time, a new data center power venture involving Microsoft and GE Vernova underscores the ongoing infrastructure buildout behind that AI ambition. The analyst community remains broadly optimistic, with a consensus "strong buy" rating and a mean price target of $561, suggesting the street sees significant long-term upside from current levels — though the wide target range ($400–$870 across 55 analysts) reflects genuine uncertainty about how quickly and profitably that AI opportunity materializes.

companies.name: Alphabet Inc
companies.summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) is sliding nearly 6% today to $346, making it one of the more notable gaps lower in the S&P 500 this session, with the stock trading well off its previous close of $368. The drop comes even as the company makes aggressive moves in the AI chip space, reportedly backing a $3.2 billion push to develop alternatives to Nvidia — a bet that could reduce long-term costs but faces execution risk in a competitive landscape. With the analyst consensus sitting at a strong buy and a mean price target of $433, the stock is trading at a roughly 25% discount to where Wall Street collectively thinks it should be, which some may see as an opportunity, though today's sharp selloff is a reminder that near-term pressure can persist regardless of longer-term targets.

companies.name: Amazon.com Inc

companies.name: Meta Platforms Inc
companies.summary: Meta shares slipped about 2.5% today to close near $563, pulling back from a previous close above $577. Analysts remain broadly bullish, with a consensus "strong buy" rating and a mean price target around $827 — roughly 47% above today's price — though the wide target range ($664 to $1,015) reflects genuine uncertainty about how bets like the India expansion will ultimately pay off.

companies.name: Tesla Inc
companies.summary: Tesla shares rose roughly 2% today, closing near $408 after trading as high as $414.75, and sit just modestly below the analyst consensus price target of around $421 — suggesting limited upside on a pure price-target basis, though the wide target range ($123 to $600) reflects how sharply divided Wall Street remains on the stock's valuation. companies.name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc
companies.summary: BRK.B edged slightly lower today, slipping $0.42 to close at $489.04 after trading in a fairly tight range between $487.88 and $492.48. The most notable headline involves NetJets — Berkshire's wholly owned private aviation subsidiary — which experienced its first fatal crash, killing a prominent Texas venture capital founder; while a single incident doesn't necessarily signal operational problems, it could draw regulatory scrutiny and raises reputational questions for a premium brand that markets heavily on safety and reliability. No analyst price target consensus is available for comparison, so investors will likely be watching for any further details on the NetJets incident and whether it generates meaningful liability exposure or affects demand for the company's fractional jet ownership services.
